reasoning: I'm voting for Biswajit Das again, but I'm not at peace about it like I was when I first voted. My dadu has his citizenship certificate now—that's real, that matters—but I watched fourteen names appear five days before polling. I know how timing works. My Kanyashree came every month, my mother's card still works, and I can see college through to my degree because of TMC schemes, not promises. Shantanu Thakur and CAA are real, yes. But if I switch now, will the schemes stop? Will the notification keep processing, or will it get slower? My dadu waited forty years; he can wait for the next batch too. I'm staying with what reaches my hand, but I'm not fooling myself that I'm choosing the right answer—I'm choosing the safer one.
primary_drivers: Scheme continuity (Kanyashree, Swasthya Sathi) that reaches her directly
Suspicion that CAA notification timing is political, not a sign of genuine follow-through
Fear that switching governments will disrupt her education and her father's employment
